mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns exceeds -0.15 in fewer than 3 of 5 rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS combined with housing starts measured by HOUST both declining month-over-month signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces equity valuations and forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-declining instances, or joint decline events occur in fewer than 8 instances per 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO rising above 100.5 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns is negative or below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS growth decelerating below 50,000 net new jobs per month combined with rising unemployment UNRATE signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer confidence and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 50,000 AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in prior month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 235 combined with federal funds rate FEDFUNDS remaining below 1% signals monetary policy lag that creates real rate compression, reducing equity risk premiums and producing positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or real rate compression does not correlate with equity outperformance (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth GDPC1 measured at quarterly frequency shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle expansion that supports equity valuations and produces positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO declining below 99.5 combined with payroll employment PAYEMS growth slowing below 75,000 monthly signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that reduces equity earnings expectations and produces negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year windows.
